suggested_titles.title: Celestial Markers
suggested_titles.subtitle: An abstract exploration of depth and light.
suggested_titles.description: A profound canvas featuring deep cobalt blue, contrasted with vibrant orange forms and delicate white lines, evoking a sense of vast space and navigational elements.

suggested_titles.title: Azure Horizons
suggested_titles.subtitle: Where deep blues meet fiery signals.
suggested_titles.description: An abstract composition dominated by an expansive azure field, punctuated by vivid red-orange shapes and subtle linear elements that suggest distant vistas or charted pathways.

suggested_titles.title: The Blue Divide
suggested_titles.subtitle: Tension and serenity in abstract forms.
suggested_titles.description: A striking abstract work presenting a rich, textured blue expanse traversed by fine white lines, with compelling red-orange blocks creating visual anchors and dynamic tension.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Presents the artwork as a focal point in a premium, contemporary exhibition space, emphasizing its structural and chromatic power through a dramatic low-angle perspective.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Minimalist contemporary art gallery with clean architectural lines and controlled proportions.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, sophisticated, and focused, with a cool, contemplative quietness, emphasizing the artwork's commanding presence.
materials: Matte off-white plastered wall with subtle texture
Polished light grey concrete floor
Simple white ceiling
Discreet white track lighting fixtures
Low, minimalist polished concrete bench
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, even gallery daylight from an unseen source, augmented by subtle, focused track lighting that gently illuminates the artwork, highlighting its surface texture and vibrant colors without harsh shadows.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Nadir Controlado: Camera is positioned very low, near the floor, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, using a controlled nadir-inspired architectural viewpoint. The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the room's geometry to be apparent while keeping the artwork dominant.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The extremely low vantage point (20-35 cm from the floor) dramatically emphasizes the verticality of the clean gallery wall and the artwork itself. The polished concrete floor plane extends dynamically into the foreground, leading the eye upward to the canvas. Architectural lines converge, creating a deep perspective and a sense of elevation for the artwork, which is seen from slightly below, preserving its rectangular form and physical presence. A low, sleek concrete bench in the foreground helps frame the scene and anchors the low perspective.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The minimalist gallery environment allows the artwork's intense blue and striking orange accents to command full attention. The clean lines and neutral palette amplify the artwork's color and abstract form, making it the undeniable protagonist. The sense of vastness and contemplation in the artwork resonates with the expansive, uncluttered space.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This presentation elevates the artwork to a museum or premium gallery exhibition standard, appealing to collectors who value a clean, architectural, and sophisticated display. The unique camera perspective creates a memorable and distinctive visual, enhancing the artwork's perceived value and artistic significance. It highlights the artwork's presence as a substantial, impactful piece.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A minimal contemporary art gallery. The artwork, a vibrant abstract painting with deep blue and bright orange forms on a white-lined surface, is precisely centered on a matte off-white plastered wall. The floor is polished light grey concrete. A low, sleek, polished concrete minimalist bench is positioned in the foreground, partially cropped, creating strong architectural lines that emphasize the very low camera perspective. Soft, even gallery daylight illuminates the space, complemented by discreet track lighting gently highlighting the artwork's textured surface. The camera is positioned very low, approximately 20-35 cm from the ground, looking upward, creating strong rising architectural verticals and a dynamic oblique view across the room with coherent vanishing lines. The artwork appears elevated and dominant against the clean wall. Preserve the exact abstract artwork from the reference image, including its real format, proportions, composition, colors, marks, and surface texture.

The floor plane, wall height, ceiling lines, columns, door frames or architectural verticals may rise strongly in the composition. Use a controlled oblique angle across the room, not a straight-on product view, with one coherent vanishing direction for architecture and artwork. Use the nadir effect mainly through the surrounding room geometry: floor lines, vertical wall planes, ceiling height, architectural edges, columns, furniture legs or door frames. The artwork remains the visual subject, but the dramatic low viewpoint is carried by the architecture rather than by warping the canvas.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Presents the artwork as a prized possession in a discerning collector's intimate and culturally rich home, elevating its status and commercial appeal.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Cultivated, introspective, and profound, with a serene yet focused intensity. The ambiance is one of quiet contemplation and curated taste, where the artwork is the cultural center.
materials: Warm limewash walls in a muted, creamy neutral tone
Wide-plank dark smoked oak flooring with a subtle sheen
A heavy, textured wool area rug in charcoal grey or deep taupe
A low-slung, minimalist console table in ebonized wood or dark bronze
Understated ceiling with discrete, recessed architectural lighting fixtures
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, filtered daylight entering from a large, unseen window to the side, casting gentle, elongated shadows on the floor and subtly highlighting the artwork's textural variations. Complemented by discreet, warm evening-mode architectural lighting that subtly illuminates the artwork, creating depth.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Contrapicado 7/8: Camera height is low, below normal knee height, with the viewpoint grounded close to the floor but still architectural. Use a 7/8 oblique rotation so the wall, artwork depth, and room volume are visible through one shared perspective. The artwork is seen with strong side depth and believable thickness; use clear asymmetry and negative space toward the vanishing side while keeping surrounding wall and floor aligned to the same camera.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing the artwork to be visually dominant while integrating it believably within the voluminous space.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The low, upward-looking angle imbues the artwork with a monumental presence, emphasizing its scale and physical canvas thickness. The 7/8 oblique view provides significant spatial depth, showcasing the artwork's integration into a sophisticated architectural setting and enhancing its tactile quality and visual weight.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep, contemplative blue and vibrant red forms resonate powerfully within the quiet, cultivated atmosphere of a collector's salon. The muted, warm backdrop allows the artwork's intense colors and expressive brushwork to command attention, fostering a meditative engagement. This setting positions the abstract piece as a central cultural artifact, inviting deep reflection.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context elevates the artwork's perceived value by presenting it within a highly curated, sophisticated domestic environment. It appeals to discerning collectors by demonstrating its ability to be a significant focal point in a refined home. The architectural camera angle enhances its premium commercial presentation, suggesting a timeless, museum-quality acquisition.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A gallery-wrapped canvas featuring an expressive abstract painting with a deep blue background, vibrant red geometric shapes, and thin white lines, is hung centrally on a muted, warm limewash wall in a quiet collector's salon. The camera captures a Contrapicado 7/8 view, low angle looking up, with a 7/8 oblique rotation, emphasizing canvas thickness and room depth. In the foreground, a dark smoked oak wide-plank floor is visible, with a minimalist, low-slung ebonized wood console table partially cropped on the left, leading the eye towards the artwork. The midground is dominated by the elevated artwork. The background shows the warm limewash wall receding into the quiet room, with subtle architectural details like a soft corner. The room maintains warm domestic neutrals, muted collector tones, and no hotel catalog gloss. Soft, filtered daylight from an unseen window on the side casts gentle highlights on the artwork's textured surface and subtle shadows on the floor and wall. No visible people or animals. Artwork must retain exact colors, composition, proportions, and physical thickness. Authentic physical canvas presentation.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To showcase the artwork's vibrant depth within an elegant, serene domestic setting, appealing to a discerning collector who values sophisticated design and natural light.
contextual_proposals.space_type: A refined living room or elegant sitting area within a contemporary residence.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Serene, aspirational, sophisticated, bathed in the soft, warm glow of late afternoon sun. Quiet luxury with a hint of dramatic contrast from the artwork.
materials: Limewash walls in a pale off-white
Polished wide-plank pale oak flooring
Subtly textured cream-colored natural fiber rug
Bleached oak console table
Subtle decorative bronze object
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, directional, polished daylight from large windows, simulating late afternoon 'golden hour' light, creating warm highlights and subtle shadows that enhance room depth. Indirect ceiling lighting subtly supplements.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Reflejo Dorado de Tarde / Palazzo: Camera height is elegant human standing height, approximately 150-170 cm, as if observed calmly from inside a palazzo room. Use a soft three-quarter rotation that catches reflected late light while keeping the artwork plane legible. The composition is stable, warm, and architectural, with golden afternoon reflection supporting the room rather than repainting the artwork; keep elegant balance but avoid perfect symmetry unless the room itself naturally demands it.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance. The artwork is prominent, but the room's depth and architectural details are visible, reinforcing the 'room-scale' context.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The soft three-quarter view highlights the artwork's physical presence and canvas thickness, preventing a flat appearance. The angle allows for a receding wall, creating spatial depth and visual interest, consistent with the Palazzo camera description. The golden light catches the wall and artwork texture, making the colors richer.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The deep blue of the artwork finds a sophisticated counterpoint in the warm neutral limewash walls and golden light, creating a dynamic yet harmonious contrast. The red-orange elements gain intensity against this refined backdrop, becoming focal points of energy. The lines in the artwork resonate with the architectural lines of a well-designed contemporary space, embodying quiet luxury.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context places the artwork in a highly aspirational yet relatable domestic environment, appealing to affluent buyers seeking art that enhances a sophisticated lifestyle. The 'Quiet Luxury Window Light' variant positions the artwork as a centerpiece within a serene, sun-drenched home, maximizing its appeal for everyday appreciation and high-end residential placement. The premium materials and lighting elevate the perceived value of the artwork.

The room features limewash walls in a pale off-white, polished wide-plank pale oak flooring, and a subtly textured cream-colored natural fiber rug. The artwork, a vertical rectangular canvas predominantly deep blue with white lines and vibrant red-orange square-like forms, is wall-hanging on a prominent wall. The camera is at elegant human standing height, approximately 160 cm, with a soft three-quarter rotation capturing reflected late afternoon golden light, creating subtle highlights and shadows. The composition shows architectural depth with a receding wall or corner. A minimalist, premium design console table in bleached oak with a single subtle decorative bronze object is positioned against the wall, partially visible in the foreground, framing the artwork. The artwork remains the undisputed visual focus of the image, physically coherent and dominant.

contextual_proposals.context_role: This context juxtaposes the artwork's vibrant modern abstraction with the enduring elegance of historical European architecture, appealing to discerning collectors who appreciate both heritage and contemporary art.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Grand Sala or Collector's Study in a European Palazzo
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: A tranquil, refined historical atmosphere with a contemporary sensibility, imbued with the warm, contemplative glow of late afternoon light filtering through tall windows.
materials: Aged plaster walls in muted cream or off-white
Dark, wide plank parquet floor with subtle wear
Restrained architectural molding and high ceilings
Dark-stained antique wooden console or low cabinet with subtle carving
Plush, low-pile rug in muted, earthy tones
Upholstered seating (e.g., low armchairs) in natural linen or velvet
Heavy linen or raw silk drapes at windows
contextual_proposals.lighting: Golden late afternoon sunlight streaming softly through a large window, casting long, gentle shadows across the floor and walls, accentuating textures and imparting a warm, ethereal glow.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Vista Aérea con Contexto de Ventanas: Camera height is clearly elevated, approximately 240-320 cm above the floor or from an upper balcony/ladder viewpoint, well above normal eye level. This must read as a high-angle architectural view, not a standing-height 3/4 room view. Use a gentle diagonal rotation so window context and the artwork wall are both visible without splitting perspectives. The room is comprehensible from above. Show a meaningful amount of floor plane, rug, table top, seating tops, and surrounding architecture; the artwork remains identifiable on the wall but the camera must clearly look downward into the room. Allow a moderate offset so floor geometry and window or light-source context carry part of the frame.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing for a comprehensive view of the room's layout and the artwork's position within it, while ensuring the artwork remains a dominant focal point.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The elevated, high-angle view articulates the grand proportions and spatial organization of the palazzo room. By looking downward, it reveals the exquisite floor details and the carefully curated furniture arrangement, grounding the artwork in a sophisticated, three-dimensional environment. The diagonal rotation ensures the interplay of light from the window, the architectural context, and the artwork on the wall are all compositionally unified, enhancing the artwork's physical presence and its integration into the space.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep, almost oceanic blue and striking red-orange forms create a powerful contrast with the aged, warm tones of the palazzo interior. This setting frames the contemporary abstract piece within a rich historical narrative, highlighting its timeless appeal and ability to activate traditional spaces. The golden late light enhances the texture and depth of the artwork's surface, establishing a harmonious yet dynamic dialogue between old and new.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This context appeals to an affluent market segment that collects modern art for historical or architecturally significant properties. It positions the artwork as a prestigious acquisition that elevates a grand interior, suggesting cultural depth and a refined aesthetic sensibility. The unique aerial perspective offers a premium, editorial-style presentation that distinguishes the artwork from standard mockups, making it highly desirable for collectors seeking distinctive pieces.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A grand historical European palazzo room bathed in warm golden late afternoon light. A large, vertically oriented abstract painting featuring a deep ultramarine blue ground with dynamic white linear elements and vibrant red-orange textured rectangular shapes, hangs prominently on an aged plaster wall. The camera is elevated (approximately 290 cm above the floor) looking downward with a 38-degree tilt and gentle diagonal rotation. This high-angle view reveals a dark wide plank parquet floor with a central arrangement: a low, dark-stained antique wooden console table with subtle carving, topped with a single unfussy large ceramic vase, and flanked by a pair of elegant, low-slung armchairs upholstered in dark linen, all visible from their top-down profiles. A plush, muted-tone rug partially covers the floor beneath the arrangement. A tall, arched window with heavy linen drapes (partially open) is visible in the background, allowing golden light to stream in, creating soft, long shadows across the floor. Tall ceilings with subtle decorative molding are implied. The artwork's physical canvas thickness is clearly visible, integrated into this sophisticated historical yet contemporary interior. No visible people or animals. Realistically scaled.

contextual_proposals.context_role: Showcases the artwork in a refined, authentic artist's studio environment, emphasizing its material quality and contemplative nature.
contextual_proposals.space_type: Artist's working studio corridor / elongated wall
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Quiet, contemplative, organized, authentic artist's studio, filled with soft, consistent daylight.
materials: Smooth painted plaster wall (off-white or light grey)
Light grey polished concrete floor
Matte white studio ceiling
Subtle black metal studio hardware or minimalist wooden studio furniture in the background
contextual_proposals.lighting: Soft, consistent north light, gently illuminating the artwork and creating subtle depth in the studio corridor, with a low contrast ambient light.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Pasillo, Obra Descentrada y Próxima: Camera height is natural standing or slightly lowered human height, close enough to feel the corridor edge. Use an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with the artwork deliberately off-center and close rather than symmetrically framed. Place the artwork on a long wall plane rather than a short frontal display wall. The wall should extend laterally into the scene like a gallery passage, corridor, or elongated architectural plane, creating a clear vanishing direction. Use floor lines, ceiling lines, wall joints, lighting tracks, windows, columns, doorways, or distant wall planes to reinforce depth. The artwork may sit off-center and relatively close to the viewer, while the architecture recedes naturally to one side. Avoid product-style centered symmetry.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, allowing for appreciation of artwork texture and depth within the studio space.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The subtle 3/4 oblique angle and the receding studio corridor highlight the artwork's physical presence and depth, making it feel integral to the refined creative environment while maintaining its role as the focal point.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep blue and textured surface find a serene counterpart in the clean, neutral tones of a refined studio. This context underscores the artwork's authenticity and its connection to the creative process, presenting it in an environment that values artistic integrity over ostentation.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: Positions the artwork within a premium, authentic artist's space, appealing to collectors who appreciate provenance and the craftsmanship inherent in a studio setting. The clean backdrop ensures the artwork's colors and textures are the primary focus, enhancing its perceived value.
contextual_proposals.mockup_prompt: A vertical abstract painting with a deep blue textured background, vivid orange-red rectangular shapes, and delicate white lines, hanging on a pristine, smooth painted plaster wall in a refined artist's studio. The artwork is positioned off-center to the left, slightly above eye-level, revealing its physical canvas thickness. The wall extends laterally to the right into a clean studio corridor, receding with architectural depth. The floor is smooth, light grey polished concrete. Soft, diffused north light enters from an unseen window, gently illuminating the artwork and creating subtle depth in the studio corridor. Subtle studio traces like a lean, minimalist wooden easel or a stack of clean canvases are visible in the distant background, without clutter. The camera is at natural standing height, capturing an offset three-quarter corridor angle, with a clear vanishing direction towards the right, making the artwork prominent but integrated into the expansive, quiet studio environment. Ensure the artwork surface, canvas edges, canvas thickness, artwork texture, and the immediate wall area around the artwork are sharp and fully legible, with a subtle natural depth-of-field falloff in the distant corridor.

contextual_proposals.context_role: To present the artwork within a highly sought-after, sophisticated midcentury design environment that emphasizes architectural depth and curated collection.
contextual_proposals.space_type: A refined, art-focused midcentury loft apartment with architectural concrete elements.
contextual_proposals.atmosphere: Sophisticated, contemplative, and art-centric. A warm, grounded midcentury aesthetic infused with a subtle, rich burgundy-toned spatial mood, creating an intimate yet expansive feeling. Architectural and collected.
materials: Vertical walnut wood paneling
Smooth, unpolished architectural concrete (structural elements, ceiling)
Polished concrete floor
Large, low-pile wool rug in deep muted burgundy
Dark leather or textural tweed for lounge chair
Minimalist dark wood for side table
Sheer linen curtains
contextual_proposals.lighting: Controlled, warm daylight filtering through large, industrial-style windows, softened by sheer linen curtains, creating gentle highlights and shadows. Strategic, focused warm spotlighting from an adjustable midcentury track light illuminates the artwork, enhancing its texture and color depth, contributing to the 'burgundy-toned spatial mood' with its warmth.
contextual_proposals.camera_view: Borgoña Recovecos / Cámara 3/4 en Loft de Hormigón: Camera height is human but tucked into an architectural recess, approximately 120-160 cm depending on the loft edge. Use a 3/4 view from a recess, corner, or architectural edge, with a burgundy-toned spatial mood and one clean vanishing direction. The artwork is partially contextualized by loft recesses and concrete architecture, creating depth and intimacy without hiding the piece; allow strong off-center framing through recesses, corners, side planes, or architectural edges.
contextual_proposals.camera_distance: Controlled architectural distance, ensuring the artwork remains dominant while providing sufficient context of the midcentury loft setting.
contextual_proposals.camera_angle_notes: The 3/4 view from an architectural recess enhances the physical presence and depth of the artwork, revealing its thickness and integrating it organically into the sophisticated, layered space. The receding lines of the loft architecture draw the eye towards the artwork, creating a dynamic and elegant composition.
contextual_proposals.human_presence: No visible people or animals. Human context expressed through architecture, culture, objects, season, and refined interior atmosphere.
contextual_proposals.curatorial_reason: The artwork's deep blues and fiery reds create a striking dialogue with the warm walnut wood and the cool concrete, while the midcentury design language provides a sophisticated, timeless frame. The abstract forms and minimalist lines of the art resonate with the clean midcentury aesthetic, positioning the piece as a valuable addition to a discerning collector's environment. The subtle burgundy tone in the rug or lighting enriches the visual experience, adding depth to the primary blues and reds.
contextual_proposals.commercial_reason: This setting elevates the artwork to a collector's item within a highly desirable midcentury modern context. It appeals to buyers seeking art that complements sophisticated, design-forward living spaces, showcasing the piece's versatility and enduring appeal. The architectural gravitas of the loft with concrete and warm wood underscores the artwork's premium status and its ability to anchor a refined interior.

This wall is part of a recessed architectural alcove within a larger open-plan space featuring exposed smooth concrete structural columns and ceiling. A low-slung, dark leather midcentury lounge chair and a minimalist dark wood side table are positioned in the foreground to the side, maintaining lower visual weight than the artwork. A large, low-pile wool rug in a deep, muted burgundy covers part of the polished concrete floor, subtly infusing a rich, warm tone. Controlled, warm daylight filters from large industrial windows, softened by sheer linen, casting soft shadows. A focused warm spotlight from an adjustable midcentury track light illuminates the artwork. View is a 3/4 angle from an architectural recess, capturing a clean vanishing direction and revealing foreground concrete and the texture of the walnut paneling. Ensure artwork physical presence, thickness, and precise colors. No visible people, animals, text, or brands.
